plan_adherence_notes: This task directory has no local plan.json, so I evaluated the diff against step 3 of .fleet/tasks/bayes-market-dej-v3-execute-step-4/plan.json. The branch backfills the missing frontend subscription/query prerequisite in frontend/src/lib/api/client.ts, frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts, and frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts, gates subscription from REST-loaded active market data in frontend/src/routes/MarketDetail.tsx, keeps the route rendering on `const m = data.market`, and lets status-driven rerenders hide active-only UI without route-local reset logic. The remaining validation gap is test execution: the current MarketDetail Vitest file is still REST-only and the local workspace has no frontend dependencies installed.
missed_questions: Should the new subscription hook explicitly no-op when `WebSocket` support is unavailable or unmocked in test/non-browser environments, so existing route tests do not depend on ambient runtime behavior?
reason: The implementation matches the researched route-thin/query-driven approach: live updates patch the React Query market cache, MarketDetail still renders from `data.market`, active-only panels disappear from the existing status gates, and reconnect/terminal handling stays in the shared hook instead of route-local state. Test execution was limited by the workspace: frontend/node_modules is absent, so `npm test -- --run frontend/src/__tests__/MarketDetail.test.tsx` failed with `vitest: Permission denied` and `npm run build` failed on missing frontend modules rather than branch-specific code.
